utterances.text: Welcome everyone. Today we want to go deep on the Salesforce integration so Vikram has a clear picture before we sign next week.
Thanks Olivia. I'm the Salesforce admin and also responsible for our data warehouse integration, so I'll have questions on both sides.
Let's start with field-level security. Our prospect record has fifteen custom fields, several of them flagged as restricted in Salesforce. How does the sync respect that?

The integration uses an OAuth connected app that authenticates as a dedicated integration user. Field-level security on that user determines exactly which fields sync. Anything restricted in Salesforce stays restricted.
Good. Second question is API limits. We already pull a daily snapshot of activity into Snowflake. Are we going to compete with the Cadenza integration for API capacity?

Salesforce API calls are shared, so yes we do consume from the same pool. We can switch heavy reads to the Salesforce bulk API on our side, which is much cheaper per record. Worth coordinating windows.
On the Cadenza side, your REST API also has a rate limit, right? We pull about 1.2 million activity records per day for our BI dashboards.

The default REST quota is 10,000 requests per minute, which 1.2 million daily fits well inside. For partner integrations we also offer an elevated quota and a bulk export endpoint that's much more efficient.
Let's talk objects. Today we use Lead, Contact, Account, Opportunity. Do you support Tasks and Campaign Member?
Lead, Contact, Account, Opportunity are first-class. Tasks are written automatically for every Cadenza activity. Campaign Member is supported through a customizable mapping that your admin sets up.
What about custom objects? We have a custom object for renewal contracts that sales managers refer to constantly.
Custom objects are supported as read-only in the Cadenza side panel today. Read-write on custom objects is on the roadmap for later this year. I can confirm a quarter once I check with the PM.
Read-only is fine for now. Last thing from me — error handling. When a sync fails on a record, how do we know?
Failed records show up in the admin sync log with the exact error code. You can also subscribe to a webhook so your data team gets a notification when error rate goes above a threshold.
Perfect. I think Vikram has everything he needs to brief our team. Vikram, agreed?
Yes, this gives me what I need. Send me the bulk API documentation and we are good.
